title: Abort after getting `approx_len` of an `AtomicDict`
author: devdanzin
body:
The code below results in an abort:

```python
import cereggii
a = cereggii.AtomicDict()

for x in range(10000):
    a[x] = x

a.approx_len()

for x in range(100):
    a[x] = x
```

Here's the backtrace:
```
python: Objects/object.c:906: clear_freelist: Assertion `freelist->size == 0 || freelist->size == -1' failed.

Program received signal SIGABRT, Aborted.

#0  __pthread_kill_implementation (threadid=<optimized out>, signo=6, no_tid=0) at ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:44

#2  __GI___pthread_kill (threadid=<optimized out>, signo=signo@entry=6) at ./nptl/pthread_kill.c:100
#3  0x00007ffff744579e in __GI_raise (sig=sig@entry=6) at ../sysdeps/posix/raise.c:26
#4  0x00007ffff74288cd in __GI_abort () at ./stdlib/abort.c:73
#5  0x00007ffff7428830 in __assert_fail_base (fmt=<optimized out>, assertion=<optimized out>, file=<optimized out>, line=<optimized out>, function=<optimized out>) at ./assert/assert.c:118
#6  0x00007ffff743be1f in __assert_fail (assertion=<optimized out>, file=<optimized out>, line=<optimized out>, function=<optimized out>) at ./assert/assert.c:127
#7  0x0000555555a53009 in clear_freelist (freelist=freelist@entry=0x5555565280d8 <_PyRuntime+379992>, is_finalization=is_finalization@entry=0,
    dofree=dofree@entry=0x555555a5838b <free_object>) at Objects/object.c:906
#8  0x0000555555a548ab in _PyObject_ClearFreeLists (freelists=freelists@entry=0x5555565280c8 <_PyRuntime+379976>, is_finalization=is_finalization@entry=0) at Objects/object.c:949
#9  0x0000555555ce1b4b in _PyGC_ClearAllFreeLists (interp=interp@entry=0x5555564eb6c0 <_PyRuntime+131648>) at Python/gc_free_threading.c:2949
#10 0x0000555555ce1f75 in gc_collect_internal (interp=0x5555564eb6c0 <_PyRuntime+131648>, state=state@entry=0x7bfff53b4080, generation=generation@entry=2) at Python/gc_free_threading.c:2229
#11 0x0000555555ce2347 in gc_collect_main (tstate=tstate@entry=0x555556523d80 <_PyRuntime+362752>, generation=generation@entry=2, reason=reason@entry=_Py_GC_REASON_MANUAL)
    at Python/gc_free_threading.c:2314
#12 0x0000555555ce28e6 in PyGC_Collect () at Python/gc_free_threading.c:2608
#13 0x0000555555d64964 in _Py_Finalize (runtime=runtime@entry=0x5555564cb480 <_PyRuntime>) at Python/pylifecycle.c:2139
#14 0x0000555555d64a64 in Py_FinalizeEx () at Python/pylifecycle.c:2266
#15 0x0000555555dd464d in Py_RunMain () at Modules/main.c:777
#16 0x0000555555dd4834 in pymain_main (args=args@entry=0x7bfff52010a0) at Modules/main.c:805
#17 0x0000555555dd4bb9 in Py_BytesMain (argc=2, argv=0x7fffffffdb88) at Modules/main.c:829
#18 0x0000555555815676 in main (argc=<optimized out>, argv=<optimized out>) at ./Programs/python.c:15
```

comments.author: dpdani
comments.body:
The `approx_len` method is due a reimplementation so I'm inclined to mark this as "won't fix".
- https://github.com/dpdani/cereggii/issues/48

I'll see if I have time to take a look anyway.
